title: ToolPresets Object
description: ToolPresets represents a collection of ToolPreset. It provides access and allows the manipulation like removing and extending the list.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates and inserts a new Preset at the end of the Preset collection of the owning Tool. The new Preset will have the same values as the owning Tool.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: itemsByName
methods.description: Search presets by name. Returns all presets for which the name matches the given pattern. Compare is case insensitive and characters * and ? are used for wild-card matching.

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Remove Preset by index from the owning Tool.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of Presets of the owning Tool.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

add.title: ToolPresets.add Method
add.description: Creates and inserts a new Preset at the end of the Preset collection of the owning Tool. The new Preset will have the same values as the owning Tool.

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

classType.title: ToolPresets.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: ToolPresets.item Method
item.return_description: Returns Preset at by given index, null otherwise

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Index of the Preset in the owning Tool that should be returned.

itemsByName.title: ToolPresets.itemsByName Method
itemsByName.description: Search presets by name. Returns all presets for which the name matches the given pattern. Compare is case insensitive and characters * and ? are used for wild-card matching.
itemsByName.return_description: Returns all presets with matching name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the Preset to search for. The string can define a pattern with wild-card matching. '*' represents an arbitrary sequence including the empty sequence and '?' represents one arbitrary character.

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

remove.title: ToolPresets.remove Method
remove.description: Remove Preset by index from the owning Tool.
remove.return_description: Returns true for successful deletion, false otherwise

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Index of the Preset in the Tool that should be removed.

count.title: ToolPresets.count Property
count.description: The number of Presets of the owning Tool.

# Get the value of the property.
